description_long: This is a large, multi-barreled rotary machine gun, commonly known as a minigun or Gatling-style weapon. The body is robust and industrial, featuring a cylindrical central housing with multiple parallel barrels extending forward, each encased in a ventilated shroud. The rear section includes a prominent pistol grip and a secondary forward grip, both designed for two-handed operation, with visible trigger mechanisms and wiring. The surface is predominantly matte black metal, with some areas showing wear and subtle metallic highlights, suggesting heavy-duty use. The front of the barrels is capped with a perforated muzzle device, likely for heat dissipation and flash suppression. The overall construction is solid and utilitarian, with exposed mechanical components and reinforced structural elements.
description: A heavy, multi-barreled rotary machine gun with a matte black metal finish, featuring a ventilated barrel shroud and dual grips for two-handed operation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rotary barrels, main housing, and both grips.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the barrels and the perforated muzzle device.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the barrel assembly, main housing, and visible wiring.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the pistol grip, trigger mechanism, and the back of the central housing.
